edge: Trust Region Policy Optimization methods represent a popular approach to stabilize the policy updates within the context of Policy Optimization algorithms.
Trust Region Policy Optimization methods usually rely on the Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence to limit the change in the policy.

The Wasserstein distance represents a natural alternative, in place of the KL divergence, to define trust regions or to regularize the objective function, within the context of Trust Region Policy Optimization methods.
We explore optimal transport discrepancies (which include the Wasserstein distance) to define trust regions within the context of the Wasserstein distance.

Empowered by our theoretic results, we propose a novel algorithm, OT-TRPO, for trust region policy optimization with optimal transport discrepancies.
Our results reveal that trust regions defined by optimal transport discrepancies can offer advantages over the KL divergence or non-trust region methods.
Our analysis bases on a one-dimensional convex dual reformulation of the optimization problem for the policy update which enjoys strong duality.
